summary: Backend testing completed for split chapters functionality. All 13 tests passed. Fixed a critical syntax error in ImportAnalysisDialog.jsx that was preventing frontend compilation. The split-chapters endpoint correctly detects ALL 17 chapters in the test manuscript (Prologue, Chapter 1-15, Epilogue) with various formats.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: ImportAnalysisDialog.jsx had duplicate else block (lines 249-264) causing syntax error - frontend was not compiling
Split chapters endpoint correctly detects 17 chapters with various formats: 'Chapter X', 'CHAPTER X', 'Chapter One', 'Chapter I', 'Prologue', 'Epilogue'
Upload-preview endpoint returns full_content without truncation (2288 chars for test file)
Fix Everything workflow includes split_chapters action and correctly calls splitAndCreateChapters API
seed_data_creation: Test projects and chapters created during testing were cleaned up automatically
context_for_next_testing_agent: Split chapters functionality fully tested. Key findings: 1) Backend split-chapters endpoint works correctly - detects 17 chapters in test manuscript with various formats (Prologue, Chapter 1-15, Epilogue), 2) Upload-preview returns full_content without truncation, 3) Fixed syntax error in ImportAnalysisDialog.jsx (duplicate else block), 4) Fix Everything workflow includes split_chapters and calls the correct API. Chapter formats tested: 'Chapter X:', 'Chapter X -', 'CHAPTER X', 'Chapter One/Two/Three', 'Chapter I/II/III', 'Part X', 'Prologue', 'Epilogue'.

backend_apis: POST /api/ai/import/split-chapters - Splits manuscript into chapters (17 chapters detected)

bugs_fixed.issue: Duplicate else block in handleFixEverything function causing syntax error
bugs_fixed.impact: Frontend was not compiling - now compiles successfully

mocked_apis: None - all AI calls use real Emergent LLM Key
